title: Account Notes
description: Freeform notes about a client — anything worth remembering that doesn't fit a Requirement or an Interaction: how they like to be addressed, personal details, preferences, quirks, soft context. The 'really get to know the client' space.

ai.purpose: Hold the soft, human knowledge about a client that makes the relationship feel personal — how to address them, personal facts, preferences, quirks — anything that doesn't belong in a structured requirement or a dated interaction.
ai.howItWorks: Each row is one freeform note, scoped to an Account and optionally tagged. Append a note whenever you learn something worth keeping about the person or the relationship.
ai.manipulation: Append a note with the account and the body. Use the optional tag for a light category (e.g. preference, personal, logistics) if it helps.

accountId.title: Account
accountId.description: The client this note is about.

note.title: Note
note.description: The freeform note (Markdown). Anything worth remembering about this client that doesn't fit a requirement or an interaction — e.g. 'address her as Viki', 'has a kid who likes candy', 'prefers calls after 2pm'.

tag.title: Tag
tag.description: Optional light category for the note (e.g. preference, personal, logistics). Freeform.

contactId.title: Contact
contactId.description: Optional — the specific person this note is about.

title: Account Stages
description: Governed lifecycle stages for an Account (lead → prospect → active client → dormant → lost). Drives colour-coded grouping and ordering.

isWon.title: Active client
isWon.description: Whether this stage means a converted/active client.

isLost.title: Lost
isLost.description: Whether this stage means the relationship is lost/dead.

title: Accounts
description: A client or prospective client organisation — the company behind a relationship (e.g. a bar, a logistics firm, a clinic). The CRM backbone: Contacts, Deals, Interactions and BRDs all hang off an Account.

ai.purpose: The company we have (or want) a relationship with. One row per organisation; people are Contacts, opportunities are Deals.
ai.howItWorks: Created when a new lead appears (often from an inbound chat session). Carries lifecycle stage, domain/industry, language and a free-form description of who they are and how they operate.
ai.manipulation: Editable by hand or by an agent. Set stageId as the relationship progresses (lead → prospect → active client → dormant/lost).

language.title: Language
language.description: Primary language the client communicates in (ISO code or name).

primaryContactId.title: Primary contact
primaryContactId.description: Main point of contact at this account.

description.title: About
description.description: Who they are and how they operate (Markdown).

title: Requirements
description: One atomic, granular requirement within a BRD — the unit that makes the spec precise and traceable. Each row is a single statement (a data entity, a field, a workflow step, a report, a metric, an integration, an agent capability, a business rule, an edge case) with its rationale, acceptance criteria, priority and status. reqKey makes re-extraction idempotent (upsert, not duplicate); sourceQuote/sourceMessageId tie it to the exact line in the source chat; linkedIssueId ties it to the work that delivers it. changeLog records mutations so the requirement is LIVING — it can change as the conversation continues.

ai.purpose: The granular, line-by-line requirements of a BRD — each independently prioritised, statused, sourced, flagged for open questions, and linkable to the delivery task.
ai.howItWorks: Belongs to a BRD via brdId. reqKey is a STABLE slug unique within the BRD; re-extraction/re-sync upserts on (brdId, reqKey) so nothing ever duplicates. categoryId/priorityId/statusId are governed lookups. needsClarification=true + openQuestion mark a point that still needs the CLIENT's answer — directly queryable (filter needsClarification=true) and rendered by the 'Open questions' window on the BRD Workspace dashboard. sourceQuote/sourceMessageId tie the row to the client's exact words; linkedIssueId to the project-manager Story/Task that delivers it; changeLog is append-only history.
ai.manipulation: Follow the 'forge-brd-extraction' skill — it is the canonical, step-by-step procedure for this collection. One row per atomic requirement. For anything the client has not settled, set needsClarification=true and put the exact question in openQuestion; when they answer, fold it into requirement/acceptanceCriteria, append a changeLog line, clear needsClarification and move proposed→confirmed. Never reuse a reqKey and never delete a row — mark it dropped/superseded (with supersededByReqKey) instead.
warnings: reqKey is the idempotency key: keep it stable across re-syncs and never reuse it for a different requirement.
Open questions are tracked ONLY via needsClarification=true + openQuestion — never bury an unresolved point in prose.
History is append-only: prefer status/flag changes + changeLog over destructive edits.

reqKey.title: Key
reqKey.description: Stable slug, unique within the BRD. The idempotency key for re-sync.

categoryId.title: Category
categoryId.description: Requirement category (governed): data model, workflow, report, integration, agent capability, business rule, edge case, role.

needsClarification.title: Needs clarification
needsClarification.description: TRUE = this requirement has an open question that must be answered by the client before it is settled. The discovery agent works from this flag: filter needsClarification=true, ask the client the openQuestion, fill the answer back in, then set it false.

openQuestion.title: Open question
openQuestion.description: The precise question to put to the client to resolve this requirement (what exactly we need them to confirm or decide). Paired with needsClarification.

rationale.title: Rationale
rationale.description: Why this is needed — the business reason (Markdown).

acceptanceCriteria.title: Acceptance criteria
acceptanceCriteria.description: How we know it is done (Markdown).

sourceQuote.title: Source quote
sourceQuote.description: The exact line(s) from the source chat this came from.

sourceMessageId.title: Source message
sourceMessageId.description: ID of the chat_session_messages row this was extracted from.

sourceSessionId.title: Source chat
sourceSessionId.description: The chat session this requirement was extracted from.

linkedIssueId.title: Delivery issue
linkedIssueId.description: Project-manager Story/Task that delivers this requirement.

supersededByReqKey.title: Superseded by
supersededByReqKey.description: reqKey of the requirement that replaced this one, if any.

changeLog.title: Change log
changeLog.description: Timestamped notes of how this requirement changed over time (living history).

title: BRD Statuses
description: Governed lifecycle statuses for a BRD (draft → in review → approved → building → delivered → superseded).

isApproved.title: Approved
isApproved.description: Whether this status means the BRD is signed off to build.

isDone.title: Delivered
isDone.description: Whether this status means the build is delivered.

title: BRDs
description: Business Requirements Document — the structured spec for what we will build for a client. The header record: domain, overview, lifecycle status, and the all-important provenance links (the source chat session it was extracted from) and forward links (the delivery Initiative and built app). The granular content lives in Requirements rows. A BRD is LIVING: lastSyncedMessageId is the watermark of how far into the source chat it has been reconciled, so the conversation can continue and the BRD stays current.

ai.purpose: The single source-of-truth spec for a client build, decomposed into granular forge_brd_requirements rows, kept LIVING against its source chat.
ai.howItWorks: One BRD per engagement, linked to its Account/Deal and to the chat it came from (sourceSessionId). lastSyncedMessageId + lastSyncedAt are the WATERMARK — how far the source chat has been reconciled; the next sync processes only chat_session_messages with _id greater than lastSyncedMessageId, never the whole thread. version bumps on each material revision. fileIds carries the client's source artefacts (their spreadsheets/exports/photos).
ai.manipulation: Follow the 'forge-brd-extraction' skill — the canonical procedure. First pass: create the header (status draft), set sourceSessionId/language/domain/overview, link source files to fileIds, then add one forge_brd_requirements row per atomic need. To keep it LIVING on re-sync: read lastSyncedMessageId, process only messages after it, upsert requirements by reqKey, link any new files, then ADVANCE lastSyncedMessageId/lastSyncedAt and bump version. Move statusId through the lifecycle draft→in_review→approved→building→delivered.
warnings: Always advance lastSyncedMessageId after a sync — it is what stops the next run reprocessing the whole chat.
Do not delete superseded requirements — mark them dropped/superseded so the history survives.
Open questions for the client live on the requirement rows (needsClarification + openQuestion), queryable as a worklist.

title.title: Title
title.description: BRD title (e.g. 'Sörház — Cash, Expense & P&L app').

overview.title: Overview
overview.description: Narrative overview: the client's world, current workflow, and the product we will build (Markdown).

language.title: Language
language.description: Language the source conversation is in.

sourceSessionId.title: Source chat
sourceSessionId.description: The chat session this BRD was extracted from (provenance — the gold thread).

lastSyncedMessageId.title: Synced through (msg)
lastSyncedMessageId.description: ID of the last chat_session_messages row reconciled into this BRD (watermark for incremental re-sync).

lastSyncedAt.title: Last synced
lastSyncedAt.description: When the BRD was last reconciled against its source chat (ISO).

appId.title: Delivered app
appId.description: The app built from this BRD.

version.title: Version
version.description: Revision counter, bumped on each material re-sync.

title: Contacts
description: A person at an Account — the human we actually talk to (owner, manager, ops lead). Contacts belong to one Account and may be the primary contact on Deals and Interactions.

ai.purpose: People we interact with, scoped to their company (Account).
ai.howItWorks: Linked to an Account via accountId. Captures role, contact channels (email/phone/telegram) and preferred language.
ai.manipulation: Create as new people appear in conversations. Mark isPrimary for the main decision-maker.

role.title: Role
role.description: Job title / role (e.g. owner, operations manager).

isPrimary.title: Primary
isPrimary.description: Whether this is the main point of contact.

title: Deal Stages
description: Governed pipeline stages for a Deal (new → discovery → BRD → proposal → building → delivered, plus lost). These are the kanban columns of the pipeline board.

isWon.title: Won
isWon.description: Whether this stage means the deal is won/in delivery.

isLost.title: Lost
isLost.description: Whether this stage means the deal is lost.

title: Deals
description: An opportunity / engagement with an Account, moving through the pipeline stages (new → discovery → BRD → proposal → building → delivered, or lost). The PSA hinge: a Deal links its Account, the BRD that scopes it, the project-manager Initiative that delivers it, and the app we ship.

ai.purpose: A single piece of potential or in-flight work for a client, tracked through a pipeline.
ai.howItWorks: Belongs to an Account. Carries a stage (governed, kanban columns), and forward links to the BRD, the delivery Initiative and the built app.
ai.manipulation: Advance stageId as the engagement progresses. Link brdId once a BRD exists; link initiativeId/appId once the build starts.

brdId.title: BRD
brdId.description: Business requirements document scoping this deal.

appId.title: Delivered app
appId.description: The app we built / are building for this deal.

title: Interaction Types
description: Governed vocabulary of touchpoint kinds (meeting, call, chat session, voice note, email, note, document). Each has an icon for the timeline.

title: Interactions
description: A single touchpoint in the relationship timeline — a meeting, call, chat session, voice note, email, document drop or note. The activity log that keeps the whole account history in one thread. Chat-session interactions link straight to the live chat_sessions record (the discovery conversations ItemAgent runs).

ai.purpose: The chronological log of everything that happened with a client — the account's memory.
ai.howItWorks: Each row is one touchpoint, typed (governed) and timestamped, scoped to an Account (and optionally a Contact/Deal). Chat discovery sessions link via chatSessionId so the source conversation is one click away.
ai.manipulation: Append a row whenever something happens. For an inbound chat, set typeId=chat and chatSessionId to the source session.

channel.title: Channel
channel.description: Where it happened (Telegram, phone, in person, email).

chatSessionId.title: Chat session
chatSessionId.description: Source chat session, when this interaction is an agent chat.

title: Requirement Categories
description: Governed categories that classify a Requirement (data model, workflow, report/metric, integration, agent capability, business rule, edge case, role/permission, UI/UX). Used to group the spec into sections.

title: Requirement Priorities
description: Governed MoSCoW priorities for a Requirement (Must / Should / Could / Won't-for-now).

title: Requirement Statuses
description: Governed statuses for a Requirement (proposed → confirmed → in build → delivered, plus deferred / dropped). Kanban columns of the requirements board.

isConfirmed.title: Confirmed
isConfirmed.description: Whether the client has confirmed this requirement.
